title: Manually Reference an Axis From the REF Screen
summary: Use the operator station HMI to navigate to the Visu_Dual_MCP screen, stop the automatic tipping cycle, open the REF screen, identify any axis whose circled R indicator is clear, and start movement of the selected axis for manual referencing.
when_to_use: Use this procedure during maintenance or recovery when an axis must be manually referenced from the operator station HMI and the REF screen indicates an axis needs referencing.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to complete undocumented referencing steps beyond starting axis movement.
Do not use this runbook when site documentation requires a different authorized role.
Do not use this runbook as a full automatic axis referencing procedure.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform the HMI navigation, stop the automatic tipping cycle, identify the axis needing reference, and start axis movement from the REF screen.

safety_notes: This procedure is in a maintenance context and involves manual movement of the end effector into position using the HMI.
The automatic tipping cycle is stopped as part of this procedure before opening the REF screen.
The source excerpt does not provide full completion or confirmation criteria for the manual referencing movement.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI
Controls for CYCLE STOP, REF, axis selection, and the – button

system_preconditions: The operator can navigate to the Visu_Dual_MCP screen using F3
The automatic tipping cycle can be stopped from the HMI
The REF screen is available from the operator station HMI

steps.title: Open the Visu_Dual_MCP screen
steps.purpose: Reach the operator station screen used to begin the manual referencing procedure.
steps.instruction: On the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_Dual_MCP" screen using F3.
steps.expected_result: The Visu_Dual_MCP screen is displayed on the operator station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station HMI shows the Visu_Dual_MCP screen and the operator can access its controls.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot reach the Visu_Dual_MCP screen or the expected HMI screen is not available.

actions.label: Navigate to Visu_Dual_MCP
actions.exact_action: Navigate to the "Visu_Dual_MCP" screen using F3.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station HMI context for manually referencing axes and access to the Visu_Dual_MCP screen.
Operator station HMI view associated with the manual referencing procedure on page 103.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Visu_Dual_MCP screen cannot be accessed from the operator station HMI.
The HMI does not match the documented maintenance procedure context.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: On the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_Dual_MCP" screen (F3).

steps.title: Stop the automatic tipping cycle
steps.purpose: Place the system in the documented state required before opening the REF screen.
steps.instruction: Press CYCLE STOP to stop the automatic tipping cycle.
steps.expected_result: The automatic tipping cycle is stopped.
steps.healthy_condition: Automatic tipping is no longer running and the operator can proceed with manual referencing actions.
steps.failure_condition: The automatic tipping cycle does not stop after pressing CYCLE STOP.

actions.label: Press CYCLE STOP
actions.exact_action: Press CYCLE STOP to stop the automatic tipping cycle.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station HMI context where CYCLE STOP is used before manual referencing.
Page 103 operator station screen associated with navigating to Visu_Dual_MCP and stopping the cycle.
CYCLE STOP control on the operator station control panel.

stop_or_escalate_if: The automatic tipping cycle does not stop.
The CYCLE STOP control is unavailable or inactive.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Press CYCLE STOP to stop the automatic tipping cycle.

steps.title: Open the REF screen
steps.purpose: Access the reference screen used to inspect axis reference status and start manual referencing movement.
steps.instruction: Press REF to open the reference screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The REF screen is displayed and axis information is visible.
steps.failure_condition: The REF screen does not open or axis information is not available.

actions.label: Press REF
actions.exact_action: Press REF to open the reference screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: REF control on the operator station control panel.
Reference screen context associated with manual axis referencing.
REF screen context used for manual axis referencing.

stop_or_escalate_if: The REF screen cannot be opened.
The REF control is unavailable or inactive.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Press REF to open the reference screen.

steps.title: Identify the axis that needs referencing
steps.purpose: Determine which axis requires manual referencing before starting movement.
steps.instruction: Check the circled "R" next to the axis information. If the circled "R" is clear, that axis needs to be referenced.
steps.expected_result: The axis requiring reference is identified from the REF screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can clearly determine whether an axis needs referencing based on the circled R indicator.
steps.failure_condition: The circled R status cannot be interpreted or the axis needing reference cannot be identified.

actions.label: Inspect circled R status
actions.exact_action: Check the circled "R" next to the axis information; if the circled "R" is clear, that axis needs to be referenced.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Axis information and the circled R reference-status indicator.
Reference-status display showing how to identify an axis that needs referencing.

stop_or_escalate_if: The circled R indicator is not visible.
The axis reference status cannot be determined from the REF screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If the circled 'R' next to the axis information is clear, that axis needs to be referenced.

steps.title: Start movement of the selected axis
steps.purpose: Begin manual axis movement for the axis identified as needing reference.
steps.instruction: Select the axis to be referenced and press the – button to start movement of the axis.
steps.expected_result: The selected axis begins movement for manual referencing.
steps.healthy_condition: The selected axis starts moving after the – button is pressed.
steps.failure_condition: The selected axis does not move after selection and pressing the – button.

actions.label: Select axis
actions.exact_action: Select the axis to be referenced.

actions.label: Start axis movement
actions.exact_action: Press the – button to start movement of the axis.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Axis selection area and minus button used to start movement.
Manual-referencing controls for selecting an axis and pressing the minus button.

stop_or_escalate_if: The selected axis does not begin movement.
The source excerpt does not provide completion criteria for the movement, so do not continue beyond the documented action without additional approved guidance.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Select the axis to be referenced and press the – button to start movement of the axis.

success_criteria: The operator reaches the Visu_Dual_MCP screen.
The automatic tipping cycle is stopped.
An axis needing reference is identified by a clear circled R indicator.
The selected axis begins movement after the – button is pressed.
healthy_conditions: The Visu_Dual_MCP screen is accessible from the operator station HMI.
The automatic tipping cycle stops when CYCLE STOP is pressed.
The REF screen opens and displays axis information.
The circled R indicator can be used to identify whether an axis needs referencing.
The selected axis starts movement when the – button is pressed.
failure_conditions: The operator cannot access the Visu_Dual_MCP screen.
The automatic tipping cycle does not stop.
The REF screen does not open.
The circled R indicator cannot be interpreted or the axis needing reference cannot be identified.
The selected axis does not begin movement.
The source does not provide completion criteria beyond starting movement.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the HMI screens or controls described in the source are unavailable.
Escalate if the automatic tipping cycle cannot be stopped with CYCLE STOP.
Escalate if the REF screen does not open or the circled R status cannot be determined.
Escalate if the selected axis does not begin movement after pressing the – button.
Stop short of inventing additional actions or confirmation checks because the source excerpt does not include completion criteria for the manual referencing movement.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: On the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_Dual_MCP" screen (F3). Press CYCLE STOP to stop the automatic tipping cycle.

Press REF to open the reference screen. If the circled 'R' next to the axis information is clear, that axis needs to be referenced. Select the axis to be referenced and press the – button to start movement of the axis.
